title: Colubrina decipiens

The classification of Colubrina decipiens is further detailed at the species level, but more research is needed for a comprehensive understanding.

intro: Colubrina decipiens, also known as the false copal or copalillo, is a type of shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Colubrina decipiens include terpenoids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Colubrina decipiens is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
